projects.subtitle: Pro Tour
projects.description:
An Olympic-style tournament broadcast with multiple events each year, streamed live on Twitch and YouTube. Four matches are recorded simultaneously to EVS machines, then scheduled into the run of show and played back while live commentators call the action.

Between rounds, we capture offline interviews with winners and play them back later off the EVS machines.

I collaborate with the EVS replay team (three operators), overseeing asset management, graphics delivery, ingest of video packages, and making sure every replay playback is accurate across the full broadcast.

media.alt: Magic: The Gathering Pro Tour broadcast control room with multiple monitors displaying live tournament feeds.

projects.subtitle: The Gotta Catch 'Em All! Game Show
projects.description:
A live trivia game show on the Pokémon Twitch channel where the game mechanics drive the content.

Contestants catch Pokémon by answering questions correctly, and each Pokémon comes with a special ability that can be used to gain an advantage or disrupt opponents. Twitch chat votes to influence what happens next. No two episodes play out the same way.

I collaborated with the engineering team to build the production system, then designed the switching controls so the show could be operated in real time as the game unfolded.

media.alt: Pokémon Gotta Catch Em All game show live production clip.

projects.subtitle: Homemade Live!
projects.description:
Three seasons of a nationally broadcast cooking show filmed in Seattle with a live studio audience. Guests have included Katie Couric, Kenji López-Alt, and Kathie Lee Gifford.

I oversaw the multi-camera department for a five-camera shoot, calling cameras live and performing a line cut that went to post-production alongside the isolated camera recordings. The goal on every shoot was making sure each camera captured unique coverage so the edit had everything it needed.

media.alt: PBS Homemade Live cooking show multi-camera production clip.

projects.subtitle: Stream of Annihilation
projects.description:
Dungeons & Dragons' first major livestream event. A two-day broadcast from Seattle featuring the largest assembly of D&D streamers ever brought together, including Matt Mercer, Joe Manganiello, Chris Perkins, and Satine Phoenix.

I assembled the crew, managed the six-figure production budget, designed the signal path and engineering plan, sourced all equipment, and ran the video switcher during both twelve-hour broadcast days.

media.alt: Dungeons and Dragons Stream of Annihilation live stream event production clip.

projects.subtitle: D&D Live 2019: The Descent
projects.description:
A three-day, 50+ hour production in Los Angeles with a main stage, four simultaneous streaming pods, and a live concert finale directed by Chris Funk of the Decemberists. The cast included Joe Manganiello, Deborah Ann Woll, Matthew Lillard, and Patrick Rothfuss.

I managed the production budget, assembled and oversaw crews for all the pods and the main stage, then produced and directed the livestream broadcast, calling cameras throughout the show.

media.alt: Dungeons and Dragons D&D Live 2019 The Descent production clip.

projects.subtitle: Hall R — *Terminator: Dark Fate* AMA
projects.description:
Reddit's first-ever live-streamed Ask Me Anything, broadcast from San Diego Comic-Con with Arnold Schwarzenegger, Linda Hamilton, and the cast of *Terminator: Dark Fate*.

We used Hovercast to connect directly to Reddit's API, pulling live posts from the platform and displaying them as on-screen graphics integrated into the broadcast. I fed cues to host Scott Rogowsky via IFB throughout the show. Produced in collaboration with Mirrored Media.

media.alt: Reddit Hall R live AMA production clip from San Diego Comic-Con.

projects.subtitle: Center Stage
projects.description:
The inaugural Fanatics Fest NYC, a three-day festival at the Javits Center that drew over 70,000 fans.

I directed cameras for the live stage show and the IMAG screens, calling audio, lighting, and talent cues for panels and live podcast recordings. All content was captured for on-demand viewing.

media.alt: Fanatics Fest NYC Center Stage live production clip.

projects.subtitle: Ludwig & Tarik Co-Stream
projects.description:
The first-ever Red Bull Home Ground North American Qualifier, a *Valorant* tournament at Fremont Studios in Seattle featuring Cloud9, Sentinels, and Shopify Rebellion competing for a spot at the World Finals.

I operated the switcher for the co-stream, a multi-camera commentary broadcast featuring Ludwig Ahgren and Tarik Çelik. During matches, they appeared picture-in-picture over the main esports feed. During lulls, they went full screen. Eight hours of live switching as the tournament unfolded.

media.alt: Red Bull Home Ground Valorant tournament co-stream production clip.

projects.subtitle: The Gotta Catch 'Em All! Game Show Live at Pokémon Worlds
projects.description:
The live stage version of the Gotta Catch 'Em All! Game Show, performed on the Programming Stage at the 2025 Pokémon World Championships in Anaheim for an audience of several hundred.

Hosted by Brian David Gilbert with three contestants, each at a podium with a physical buzzer system that connected directly to our broadcast. I directed six cameras for IMAG and recording as the game's software drove the show in real time.

media.alt: Pokémon Gotta Catch Em All game show live stage performance at Pokémon World Championships 2025.

projects.subtitle: Creator Rewards Program | Education Series
projects.description:
An ongoing live stream series produced for members of TikTok's Creator Rewards Program, running several times a week over the course of multiple months. Established TikTok creators joined as guests to share how they built their careers on the platform, covering topics like brand partnerships, long-form storytelling, monetizing content, and production standards.

Remote talent joined through Zoom and was brought into vMix for the live broadcast, which streamed to a custom landing page accessible only to invited community members. Through Hovercast's interactive platform, the audience could participate via an interactive map, trivia questions, live polls, and Q&A alongside the stream.

Produced in partnership with Hovercast.

media.alt: TikTok Creator Rewards Program live stream production clip.

projects.description:
The 4th annual Anime Awards, co-hosted by WWE Superstar Xavier Woods and Crunchyroll's Tim Lyu. Nineteen categories broadcast live on Twitch and YouTube in eight languages, with presenters including LilyPichu, SungWon Cho, and King Vader.

I directed cameras and operated the switcher for the live broadcast, collaborating with the camera department, video playback, and graphics team throughout the show.

media.alt: Crunchyroll Anime Awards live ceremony production clip.

projects.subtitle: *Throne and Liberty* — "Siege the Day"
projects.description:
A two-day live stream launching Amazon Games' MMORPG *Throne and Liberty*, broadcast from their Seattle studio for North American and European audiences. Hosted by PancakepowTV with shoutcaster Notgrenton.

The centerpiece was Castle Siege, one of the few game modes in online gaming where thousands of players fight simultaneously on a single server. Covering that required 16 observer machines, each one a virtual camera inside the game world fed into our switcher. I directed the broadcast as the hosts and commentators called the action from the studio.

media.alt: Throne and Liberty Siege the Day live stream production clip.

projects.subtitle: Pit Crew
projects.description:
An Intel-sponsored competition show on Amazon's Crown Channel, hosted by Trisha Hershberger and Malik Forté.

The concept: pro gamers face off against amateurs in popular competitive video games. Between rounds, the pros' hardware gets downgraded while the amateurs receive upgrades. By the final round, the conditions have completely flipped. The production had to blend esports gameplay coverage with live entertainment segments, adapting as the balance shifted throughout the show.

I operated the switcher in vMix and designed and built the custom switching controls using Bitfocus Companion on Elgato Stream Decks.

media.alt: Pit Crew gaming competition show production clip.

projects.subtitle: Good @ Stuff
projects.description:
A long-form arts and crafts live stream on Amazon's Crown Channel, hosted by Gina DeVivo. Guests included Doug Jones, AEW wrestler Nyla Rose, and actor Todd Stashwick.

I produced the first season, shaping the format and pacing for a show built around real-time collaboration with Twitch chat.

In the featured clip, the talent started with a blank game board and created an entire game live on stream, drawing from their personal experiences and suggestions from the audience. For the second half of the episode, they played through the entire game they had just created.

media.alt: Good at Stuff arts and crafts live stream production clip.

projects.subtitle: ChefSteps Live!
projects.description:
I directed and produced six live cooking classes for ChefSteps, a YouTube channel with over a million subscribers, designed to encourage viewers of their free content to sign up for the exclusive Studio Pass membership.

We highlighted the benefits of membership by inviting select Studio Pass members to appear on camera, adding exclusivity and sparking curiosity. Each class, covering everything from Thanksgiving turkey to Valentine's chocolate steak, featured interactive polls and responses to questions via live chat, allowing viewers to influence the sessions.

media.alt: ChefSteps Live cooking class live stream production.

projects.subtitle: Coffee Classes
projects.description:
I directed and produced 12 interactive coffee classes for Williams-Sonoma, designed for new espresso machine owners, turning their kitchens into a collaborative learning experience.

Led by 2019 US Barista Champion Sam Spillman, the classes covered everything from grinding beans to latte art. Participants didn't just get a lecture about coffee techniques; they actively shared their creations and received live feedback from Sam in real time through a custom Zoom integration, fostering an interactive and supportive environment.

media.alt: Williams-Sonoma interactive coffee class live stream production.

projects.subtitle: Live from CrateKitchen
projects.description:
At CrateKitchen in Northbrook, IL, I directed and produced nine live sessions: three cooking classes and six espresso classes.

We collaborated with renowned chefs and baristas, including Carolina Gellen, *Top Chef*'s Nini Nguyen, and 2020 US Barista Champion Andrea Allen. These experts shared invaluable tips and tricks while showcasing Crate & Barrel's most popular products.

While most of the audience watched on YouTube, we offered a VIP exclusive experience in Zoom for select community members. These Zoom attendees could interact directly with the chefs and baristas and get feedback as they followed along at home. At the end of each class, we held a product giveaway specifically for the Zoom audience.

media.alt: Crate and Barrel CrateKitchen live cooking and espresso class production.

projects.description:
An interactive gaming channel on Samsung Smart TVs where viewers watch a live host, play along from their phones, and interact through chat and polls powered by Hovercast.

I designed and built the complete production system from my home office in Seattle for their studio in Philadelphia. Gear recommendations for what the team needed to purchase, signal path design, a full vMix project, and Stream Deck controls programmed through Bitfocus Companion. Every design decision came back to one question: what does the operator need to know, see, and do in the moment they need to do it? I trained the team to operate the full system independently, then consulted remotely during their first pilot broadcasts.

media.alt: GAMELOOP gaming channel studio setup for Samsung Smart TV broadcasting.

projects.subtitle: Building Community Through Live Streaming
projects.description:
A graduate practicum I designed for the Communication Leadership program, taught every winter quarter since 2024. The course explores the difference between live streaming as traditional event coverage and the conversational, community-focused approach of creators on platforms like Twitch and YouTube.

Students design and produce their own live streams, experimenting with strategies that invite participation and make audiences feel seen and heard. Throughout the quarter, they apply these principles to projects tied to their own professional roles. The course culminates in a team production: a live stream for Comm Lead's annual Connects conference.

The curriculum translates what I've learned from professional multi-camera production into tools and frameworks that marketing and communications professionals can apply, from simple webcam setups to productions with professional crews and equipment.

media.alt: University of Washington Building Community Through Live Streaming course materials.

projects.subtitle: Office of Public Lectures
projects.description:
When the pandemic shut down the Office of Public Lectures' in-person programming, I helped them pivot to an entirely virtual operation. Over the course of the pandemic, I produced and directed 40+ live stream episodes across multiple series and shows.

I built out virtual production workflows on AWS EC2 instances and developed techniques for pulling NDI feeds out of Zoom Rooms and Zoom ISO, pioneering remote production methods that didn't exist yet at the time. The flagship series, "Coexisting with Covid-19," brought 15 episodes of academic discussion to audiences in 47 states and 33 countries across six continents.

media.alt: University of Washington Office of Public Lectures live stream programming production clip.

projects.subtitle: Truth in Education
projects.description:
A 24-hour continuous live stream designed to counter misinformation and empower voters in Florida. I managed the production from end to end: budget, crew, resources, and the full technical build. I partnered with an engineering team to design a virtual production workflow on AWS, then coordinated two crews across time zones to cover the broadcast. A US crew worked an eight-hour shift, a European crew took over for the next eight, and the US crew returned to close it out.

Over the course of the broadcast, we worked with 60+ remote guests and delivered 32 on-demand sessions and 72 social media clips produced in real time during the event.

media.alt: Truth in Education 24-hour live stream production clip.

projects.brand: NNLM / NIH All of Us Research Program
projects.description:
When the pandemic shut down in-person programming, I helped NNLM pivot to virtual production, producing and directing 30+ remote live stream events. I built out virtual workflows on AWS and developed techniques for pulling NDI feeds out of Zoom.

One highlight was "Life Interrupted," a virtual screening series pairing a breast cancer documentary with live panel discussions featuring oncologists, researchers, and survivors. Each screening was customized for a different community, partnering with organizations like the Black Women's Health Imperative, the Asian Health Coalition, and the National Association of Hispanic Nurses.

media.alt: NNLM All of Us Research Program live stream production clip.

projects.subtitle: Veep & West Wing Reunion
projects.description:
A virtual reunion bringing together Bradley Whitford, Julia Louis-Dreyfus, and Martin Sheen for a live stream fundraiser that raised over $700,000 during the broadcast.

Produced in partnership with Hovercast, coordinating celebrity talent across multiple remote locations for a high-stakes political event.

media.alt: Veep and West Wing reunion virtual fundraiser production clip.
